user_message:
<subagent_notification>
{"agent_path":"019ee21d-e51f-73a0-bac9-4f7ee4254940","status":{"completed":"Here is the read-only docs/config/decision-surface snapshot for `/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext`.

## Relevant files and why they matter

- [README.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/README.md): Primary user-facing spec + architecture overview, command inventory, MCP tool/docs contract language, hook behavior summary.
- [CLAUDE.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/CLAUDE.md): Runtime architecture and process baseline, review principles, MCP/decision-memory behavior (primary technical contract).
- [AGENTS.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/AGENTS.md): Workflow/governance layer; codifies verification expectations, commit/PR and ADR expectations, decision/lesson reuse rules, and mandatory documentation policy for behavior changes.
- [ROADMAP.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/ROADMAP.md): Historical and near-term program direction; v0.10.0 targets (lesson reuse + inference path) are explicitly called out.
- [CHANGELOG.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/CHANGELOG.md): Release-oriented truth source for shipped vs. planned behavior; includes an `Unreleased` section with high-risk doc+behavior changes in flight.
- [LESSONS.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/LESSONS.md): Decision outcome evidence used for future risk-avoidance and planning; documents recurring themes relevant to docs/process quality.
- [docs/decisions_outcomes.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/decisions_outcomes.md): Canonical definition of outcome semantics, CLI/MCP validity, and behavior contracts.
- [docs/adr/README.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/adr/README.md): ADR convention boundary and naming model.
- [docs/adr/0001-adr-process.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/adr/0001-adr-process.md), [docs/adr/0002-mypy-strict-gradual.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/adr/0002-mypy-strict-gradual.md), [docs/adr/0003-sessions-ended-non-monotonic.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/adr/0003-sessions-ended-non-monotonic.md): Cross-cutting policy decisions.
- [docs/spec.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/spec.md): Base implementation-aligned project spec (status tags included).
- [docs/spec-ec-sync-no-filter.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/spec-ec-sync-no-filter.md): Concrete spec gap/behavior contract around `ec sync --no-filter`.
- [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-design.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-design.md) and [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-implementation.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-implementation.md): Design + execution framing for v0.10.0 doc/behavior targets.
- [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-09-dev-process-conventions.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-09-dev-process-conventions.md): Process architecture and traceability policy roots.
- [docs/templates/entirecontext-maintainer-decision-reuse-template.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/templates/entirecontext-maintainer-decision-reuse-template.md), [docs/templates/entirecontext-user-decision-reuse-template.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/templates/entirecontext-user-decision-reuse-template.md), [docs/templates/entirecontext-proactive-guidance.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/docs/templates/entirecontext-proactive-guidance.md): Reusable AGENTS/integration language patterns.
- [pyproject.toml](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/pyproject.toml): Hard policy/config surface in code form (dependencies, mypy strict policy, grandparent overrides).
- [.entirecontext/config.toml](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/.entirecontext/config.toml): Local runtime overrides currently active for this repo.
- [.omc/RELEASE_RULE.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/.omc/RELEASE_RULE.md): Release gate text, check ordering, and Codex review checklist.
- [CLAUDE.local.md](/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/CLAUDE.local.md): repo-local dogfooding targets and non-default thresholds.

## Existing docs patterns (notable)

- **Hierarchy pattern:** user-facing docs (`README.md`), implementation guidance (`docs/spec*`, `docs/superpowers/*`), policy (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`), durable decisions (`docs/adr/*`), lessons (`LESSONS.md`).
- **Traceability expectation:** multiple docs explicitly reference a behavior chain of context (`docs` → ADR/decision records → plan/implementation), surfaced in AGENTS and process plans.
- **Decision/lesson reuse is mandatory in policy:** AGENTS and templates require relevance checks, explicit conflict handling, and final reporting of decision usage when non-trivial work touches behavior/telemetry/lifecycle/search/decision memory subsystems.
- **Measure-first in docs culture:** explicitly present in AGENTS and reflected in roadmap retro policy and v0.10.0 plans.
- **Spec-to-doc contract tests:** docs mention concrete CLI call-path contracts (e.g., `spec-ec-sync-no-filter`) and expected behavior checks.

## Planning inputs surfaced for documentation-focused work

- Current active planning context is v0.10.0 close-loop work in [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-design.md] and implementation checklist [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-implementation.md], including carry-forward cleanup items and risk mitigations.
- Retro and risk carry-forward are explicit in [ROADMAP.md] and [CHANGELOG.md](Unreleased), with lesson/maturity goals and session-end auto-apply context.
- Security/compliance of docs surfaces: `README.md` is the single user-facing canonical docs index; ADRs cover cross-cutting policy; CHANGELOG is source for shipped claims.

## Risks / unknowns observed

- **Reference gap:** AGENTS/related guidance references `docs/guidance-schema.md`, but that path is not present in `docs/` in this tree (lookup returned no such file).
- **Version drift signal:** `README.md` badge still shows `0.6.0` while `pyproject.toml` and `CHANGELOG.md` indicate `0.9.3`; this creates a documentation consistency risk for readers and release-note alignment.
- **Config surface mismatch risk:** repository-local `.entirecontext/config.toml` is sparse; AGENTS/CLAUDE mention broader config dimensions, so docs should clearly distinguish defaults, optional overrides, and full runtime key set.
- **Implementation-vs-doc timing:** several high-impact changes are in `Unreleased`/plan documents (lesson surfacing, outcome-type inference); any docs update should separate shipped behavior from planned behavior explicitly."}}
</subagent_notification>
assistant_summary: {"relevant_files":[{"path":"README.md","reason":"Primary user-facing documentation surface with project overview, quick start, CLI reference, MCP tools, hook system, configuration, sync policy, and architecture claims."},{"path":"CLAUDE.md","reason":"Compact project reference for architecture, data model, hook behavior, config, tests, and review principles; useful as a current technical-doc baseline."},{"path":"AGENTS.md","reason":"Repository workflow contract covering documentation expectations, decision/lesson reuse, ADR traceability, measure-first policy, and test verification scope."},{"path":"pyproject.toml","reason":"Authoritative package metadata and tooling config: version 0.9.3, Python >=3.12, `ec` entrypoint, optional extras, pytest path, ruff config, and mypy strict overrides."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/__init__.py","reason":"Runtime version source currently set to 0.9.3, which conflicts with the README badge showing 0.6.0."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/cli/__init__.py","reason":"Canonical CLI registration surface; imports command modules and registers them with the Typer app."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/mcp/server.py","reason":"MCP bootstrap and exported tool list; documents the agent-facing protocol surface and optional `entirecontext[mcp]` dependency behavior."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/mcp/tools/search.py","reason":"Search, related-context, AST search, and activation MCP tool contracts for docs/API accuracy checks."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/mcp/tools/session.py","reason":"Session/context MCP APIs, including attribution, context apply, and turn-content surfaces."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/db/schema.py","reason":"Database schema source of truth, including `SCHEMA_VERSION = 14` and core table definitions."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/db/migration.py","reason":"Schema bootstrap and migration orchestration, relevant to setup, upgrades, and operational docs."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/db/connection.py","reason":"Local/global database path and SQLite connection behavior that documentation should not contradict."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/core/context.py","reason":"Repo/global context managers and transaction helper; important for architecture and development workflow documentation."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/hooks/handler.py","reason":"Hook dispatch matrix for Claude/Codex lifecycle events and PostCommit handling."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/hooks/session_lifecycle.py","reason":"SessionStart/SessionEnd behavior, summary/AAR/assessment catch-up side effects, and lifecycle caveats."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/hooks/decision_hooks.py","reason":"Decision surfacing/extraction hook behavior and fallback artifact conventions."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/sync/engine.py","reason":"High-level sync/pull orchestration and result shape used by CLI and operational docs."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/sync/coordinator.py","reason":"Sync control flow and artifact merge coordination behind the documented shadow-branch behavior."},{"path":"src/entirecontext/sync/__init__.py","reason":"Compatibility exports that may affect how docs describe sync module boundaries."},{"path":"tests/conftest.py","reason":"Shared test fixtures use real git repos and isolated global DBs; confirms testing workflow constraints."},{"path":"tests/conftest_hypothesis.py","reason":"Hypothesis support fixture imported by test setup; relevant for test environment documentation."},{"path":"tests/fixtures/perf_decisions.py","reason":"Fixture data for decision/PDI performance tests."},{"path":".github/workflows/ci.yml","reason":"CI verification source for expected repo-native commands and Python version matrix."},{"path":"docs/spec.md","reason":"Implementation-aligned spec snapshot, but contains drift such as schema version 6 and older MCP tool counts."},{"path":"docs/spec-ec-sync-no-filter.md","reason":"Specific sync behavior contract around `ec sync --no-filter`."},{"path":"docs/decisions_outcomes.md","reason":"Canonical documentation for the five decision outcome types and CLI/MCP validity."},{"path":"docs/adr/README.md","reason":"ADR naming, status lifecycle, and template convention."},{"path":"docs/adr/0001-adr-process.md","reason":"Accepted policy that durable cross-cutting decisions belong in `docs/adr/` while lightweight decisions remain EC records."},{"path":"docs/adr/0002-mypy-strict-gradual.md","reason":"Accepted policy for strict mypy with grandfathered override list in `pyproject.toml`."},{"path":"docs/adr/0003-sessions-ended-non-monotonic.md","reason":"Accepted won’t-fix operational caveat for dashboard maturity metrics and Codex session reopening."},{"path":"ROADMAP.md","reason":"Product direction and version history for capture/distill/retrieve/intervene loop, shipped milestones, and carry-forward work."},{"path":"CHANGELOG.md","reason":"Release-oriented source for shipped and unreleased behavior; needed to separate current behavior from in-flight docs."},{"path":"LESSONS.md","reason":"Generated lessons and assessment feedback that inform documentation risk themes and process guidance."},{"path":"docs/templates/entirecontext-maintainer-decision-reuse-template.md","reason":"Reusable maintainer guidance for decision reuse language and final reporting conventions."},{"path":"docs/templates/entirecontext-user-decision-reuse-template.md","reason":"Reusable user guidance template for integrating EntireContext decision reuse."},{"path":"docs/templates/entirecontext-proactive-guidance.md","reason":"Broader proactive memory-reuse template covering decisions, lessons, checkpoints, and attribution."},{"path":"docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-design.md","reason":"Recent design context for lesson surfacing and outcome inference; should be treated as planning/current-unreleased context."},{"path":"docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-v0.10.0-implementation.md","reason":"Recent implementation-plan context for v0.10.0 documentation/behavior targets."},{"path":"docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-09-dev-process-conventions.md","reason":"Process architecture and traceability-policy roots for docs/contribution guidance."},{"path":"CLAUDE.local.md","reason":"Repo-local dogfooding targets and non-default thresholds that may affect operational docs."},{"path":".entirecontext/config.toml","reason":"Sparse repo-local runtime overrides; docs should distinguish local overrides from defaults and full config surface."},{"path":".omc/RELEASE_RULE.md","reason":"Release gate and Codex review checklist referenced by release/process documentation."}],"existing_patterns":["Docs are layered by audience and durability: `README.md` for user-facing overview/reference, `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` for agent/process contracts, `docs/spec*` for implementation specs, `docs/adr/*` for durable decisions, `ROADMAP.md` and `CHANGELOG.md` for timeline/state, and `LESSONS.md` for assessment-derived guidance.","CLI modules register through `src/entirecontext/cli/__init__.py`; command modules expose `register(app)` rather than owning the top-level Typer app.","MCP bootstrap lives in `src/entirecontext/mcp/server.py`, while protocol tool groups are split under `src/entirecontext/mcp/tools/*.py`; documentation should keep CLI and MCP surfaces distinct.","Core services are shared by CLI, hooks, and MCP; `src/entirecontext/core/context.py` owns repo/global context and transaction boundaries.","Persistence documentation should use `src/entirecontext/db/schema.py` as the schema-version source of truth and `src/entirecontext/db/migration.py` for migration behavior.","Hooks are lifecycle/event infrastructure: `hook_cmds` funnels payloads to `hooks/handler.py`, which dispatches to capture, lifecycle, and decision hook modules with side effects and fallback artifacts.","Sync is a bounded subsystem: CLI sync/pull commands delegate to `sync.engine`, which coordinates shadow-branch export/import and artifact merge logic.","Tests use real git repositories and isolated DB fixtures from `tests/conftest.py`; this is an established convention worth documenting for contributors.","Project process expects behavior changes to connect Spec or docs, ADR or EC decision when durable, plan, code, and verification evidence.","ADR files follow sequential `NNNN-short-title.md` naming and the status lifecycle documented in `docs/adr/README.md`.","Mypy strict mode is globally enabled, with legacy modules grandfathered via the override list in `pyproject.toml`; new non-overridden modules should be strict.","Release and roadmap docs distinguish shipped versions from `Unreleased`; planned v0.10.0 behavior should not be documented as shipped unless verified in current code."],"planning_inputs":["AC-1 likely maps to improving or adding a consolidated docs entrypoint that covers overview, setup, common commands, architecture/module map, development workflow, testing, contribution guidance, and operational conventions.","AC-2 should verify commands and claims against `pyproject.toml`, `src/entirecontext/cli/__init__.py`, `src/entirecontext/mcp/server.py`, `src/entirecontext/db/schema.py`, CI config, and current docs.","AC-3 should include a stale-doc review of `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/spec.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and ADR/template docs.","The README badge version is stale relative to `pyproject.toml` and `src/entirecontext/__init__.py`; this is a concrete docs-fix candidate for a later edit phase.","`docs/spec.md` appears stale for schema version and MCP tool count; a later implementation phase should either update it or clearly label it historical/outdated.","`AGENTS.md` references `docs/guidance-schema.md`, but that file is absent from the repo file list; later planning should decide whether to add the referenced doc or adjust the reference.","Documentation should clearly separate shipped behavior from `CHANGELOG.md` `Unreleased` and `docs/superpowers/*` planned behavior.","Documentation should distinguish default config, repo-local `.entirecontext/config.toml` overrides, and optional extras such as `semantic` and `mcp`.","Because the task is documentation-only, validation should emphasize text/content review plus repo-native static/test commands only where they add confidence; no source-code behavior changes are needed.","The later review outcome can be a dedicated review note or applied doc fixes, depending on the implementation phase authority; current research should not choose that final surface.","When documenting tests, include that existing module tests should run when source modules change, while docs-only changes have no configured markdown lint/link-check command.","If documentation touches CLI/MCP command inventories, compare against live registration surfaces rather than copying old tables from stale docs.","If documentation touches operational hooks, avoid overstating synchronous guarantees because hook behavior includes async/fallback/error-telemetry paths.","If documentation touches release process, include `.omc/RELEASE_RULE.md` and `CHANGELOG.md` as evidence sources."],"test_commands":["uv run pytest","uv run pytest tests/test_core.py","uv run pytest -k \"test_search\"","uv run pytest --cov=entirecontext","uv run ruff check .","uv run ruff format .","uv run mypy src/entirecontext/"],"risks":["Documentation drift is already visible: README badge says 0.6.0 while package/runtime version is 0.9.3.","`docs/spec.md` contains older implementation claims, including schema version 6 and a smaller MCP tool surface, while code reports schema version 14 and newer MCP exports.","`AGENTS.md` references `docs/guidance-schema.md`, which was not found in the current repository file list.","No repo-native markdown lint, link checker, or docs-specific CI command was found, so docs review acceptance may need manual evidence rather than an automated docs test.","The test suite is broad and DB/e2e-heavy; full `uv run pytest` may be expensive for a docs-only change, though it is repo-native.","Hook and sync behavior include side effects, async workers, compatibility exports, and fallback paths; simplified docs could become inaccurate quickly.","`CHANGELOG.md` includes `Unreleased` items and v0.10.0 planning docs exist, so later docs must avoid presenting planned behavior as current shipped behavior.","Local untracked files exist in the worktree (`.agent-loop/`, `.air/`, `EntireContext_Intro.pptx`); later edit/review phases should avoid sweeping them into documentation changes."],"unknowns":["Which documentation surface should be considered the final deliverable: README-only, README plus docs tree, a new docs overview, or a separate review report.","Whether the later implementation phase is authorized to edit documentation files directly or should only produce review notes.","Whether `docs/guidance-schema.md` is intentionally generated/omitted, missing from this checkout, or a stale reference.","Whether `docs/spec.md` should be fully refreshed to current implementation state or converted into a historical snapshot with clear status labeling.","Whether `EntireContext_Intro.pptx` is intended to be part of project documentation or is unrelated untracked material.","Whether docs should describe only released 0.9.3 behavior or also include the `Unreleased` v0.10.0 work as forward-looking material."],"subagent_effective_count":3,"subagent_roles":[{"role_id":"implementation_surfaces","role_label":"Implementation Surfaces","summary":"Mapped CLI, MCP, core, DB, sync, and hooks boundaries plus key source files that documentation should verify against.","coverage_status":"used","key_files":["pyproject.toml","src/entirecontext/cli/__init__.py","src/entirecontext/mcp/server.py","src/entirecontext/db/schema.py","src/entirecontext/hooks/handler.py","src/entirecontext/sync/engine.py"],"gaps":["Did not inspect every command module or MCP tool implementation line-by-line; focused on boundary and source-of-truth files."]},{"role_id":"tests_and_verification","role_label":"Tests And Verification","summary":"Mapped pytest/ruff/mypy configuration, CI commands, fixture conventions, and absence of docs-specific verification tooling.","coverage_status":"used","key_files":["pyproject.toml",".github/workflows/ci.yml","tests/conftest.py","tests/conftest_hypothesis.py","tests/fixtures/perf_decisions.py"],"gaps":["No docs-specific markdown or link-check command was found."]},{"role_id":"docs_config_risks","role_label":"Docs, Config, Risks","summary":"Mapped existing documentation hierarchy, ADR/process conventions, roadmap/changelog state, templates, local config, and concrete documentation drift risks.","coverage_status":"used","key_files":["README.md","CLAUDE.md","AGENTS.md","docs/spec.md","docs/decisions_outcomes.md","docs/adr/README.md","ROADMAP.md","CHANGELOG.md","LESSONS.md"],"gaps":["Could not resolve missing `docs/guidance-schema.md`; later phase should decide whether to add it or correct the reference."]}]}
